viewsInfo.description: Entry view for building and refining natural-language concept queries before downstream SAE retrieval and feature exploration.

quotes: Concept Query View serves as the entry point for feature exploration.
A similarity distribution chart of the top 2,000 features provides an initial overview.
evidence.reasoning: The caption, interface crop, and scenario text consistently describe this as the query-entry and refinement view.

subViews.description: Upper control area where users enter a concept query, trigger search, and apply suggestion chips to refine ambiguous wording.

quotes: Users describe concepts of interest using natural language.
This view supports query refinement through suggestions by GPT-4o.
evidence.reasoning: The crop shows a text input, search button, and recommendation chips; the passages describe query entry and refinement suggestions.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Natural-language query entry form with recommendation chips used to rewrite or expand the concept query.

quotes: Concept queries need to be rephrased.
The view supports query refinement through suggestions by GPT-4o.
evidence.reasoning: The interaction is centered on entering and refining a text query rather than inspecting plotted marks.

processingSteps.name: Concept query embedding
processingSteps.description: Encodes the user query into the same semantic vector space as feature explanations so ConceptViz can compare concepts against SAE features.

processingSteps.name: GPT-4o query suggestion
processingSteps.description: Generates refinement suggestions that help users disambiguate or broaden the current concept query.

quotes: We calculate semantic similarity between user queries and SAE features using OpenAI's text-embedding-3-large model.
This view supports query refinement through suggestions by GPT-4o.
evidence.reasoning: The query input is directly supported by embedding-based matching and suggestion generation.

capabilities.description: Enter a natural-language concept and run the search against all feature explanations.

quotes: Users describe concepts of interest using natural language.
evidence.reasoning: The input box and query button support search submission.

capabilities.description: Apply or edit suggested phrasings to resolve ambiguity and improve concept search quality.

quotes: The system should provide intuitive visual feedback on query quality and suggest optimization alternatives.
The user refines their search.
evidence.reasoning: The interface shows recommendation chips and the scenarios describe iterative query rewriting.

subViews.description: Lower chart area comparing the similarity distributions of the original and optimized queries over the highest-ranked features.

quotes: A similarity distribution chart of the top 2,000 features provides an initial overview of the match.
Displaying the distribution graph.
evidence.reasoning: Both text and the crop show a chart contrasting original and optimized query behavior.

x.description: Similarity score bins or thresholds along the horizontal axis.

y.description: Number of features matching each similarity level.

color.description: Distinguishes the original query from the optimized query.

quotes: A similarity distribution chart of the top 2,000 features provides an initial overview.
The system calculates semantic similarity between feature explanations and both original and optimized queries, displaying the distribution graph.
evidence.reasoning: The chart is clearly a two-series quantitative distribution view.

capabilities.description: Shows how the original and optimized query formulations differ in the number of highly similar features.

quotes: The system should provide intuitive visual feedback on query quality.
Observing higher similarity counts with the optimized query.
evidence.reasoning: The distribution chart is the view's primary feedback mechanism for query quality.

viewsInfo.description: Layer-oriented view for identifying which SAE model is most relevant to the active concept query.

quotes: SAE Discovery View enables users to select appropriate SAE models based on concept-relevance metrics.
The overview bar above in this view displays the distribution of concept-relevant features across different network layers.
evidence.reasoning: The paper consistently describes this view as the place for cross-layer recommendation and final SAE selection.

subViews.description: Top overview strip summarizing concept-relevant feature counts across layers for multiple top-K thresholds.

quotes: The overview bar above in this view displays the distribution of concept-relevant features across different network layers.
The visualization reveals stronger semantic matches concentrated in the earlier layers.
evidence.reasoning: The crop shows a compact per-layer bar strip above the table, matching the passage description.

color.description: Color intensity reflects how strongly a layer matches the current concept.

quotes: The overview bar above in this view displays the distribution of concept-relevant features across different network layers.
evidence.reasoning: The overview is shown as a bar-like strip with per-layer color intensity.

capabilities.description: Shows where concept-relevant features appear across the model's layers and across different top-K metrics.

quotes: This layer-based organization helps users identify SAEs that balance model architectural considerations with concept relevance.
evidence.reasoning: The overview provides the cross-layer summary needed before choosing an SAE.

subViews.description: Scrollable table listing layers and Top-10, Top-100, and Top-1000 scores for direct SAE model selection.

quotes: Presenting Top 10, Top 100, and Top 1000 scores for each layer in the list below.
Users can directly select the SAE of interest from this view.
evidence.reasoning: The lower portion of the crop is a ranked table used for selection.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Tabular ranking of SAE layers with per-threshold relevance metrics used to pick the active SAE model.

quotes: Top 10, Top 100, and Top 1000 scores for each layer in the list below.
evidence.reasoning: The interface region is best characterized as a data table rather than a plotted visualization.

processingSteps.name: Multi-threshold SAE ranking
processingSteps.description: Ranks SAE models by averaging their positions across Top-10, Top-100, and Top-1000 concept-similarity counts.
input: query-feature cosine similarities across all SAE models

quotes: We then identify which SAE models contribute the most features to the Top-K most similar features, where K = {10, 100, 1000}.
This approach prioritizes SAE models that consistently rank well across different thresholds.
evidence.reasoning: These passages describe the ranking process that populates the discovery table and overview.

capabilities.description: Choose a layer-specific SAE model for downstream feature exploration.

quotes: Users can directly select the SAE of interest from this view to proceed with feature exploration.
Based on this insight, the user selects the fourth layer SAE.
evidence.reasoning: Model selection is the core interactive affordance of this table.

viewsInfo.description: Global semantic map of SAE features used to browse clusters, locate query-relevant regions, and choose features for detailed analysis.

quotes: Feature Explorer View presents a global visualization of SAE features.
All feature explanation vectors are mapped to a two-dimensional semantic space.
The system highlights query-relevant features and represents the query's position with a red pin.
evidence.reasoning: The caption, methods section, and usage scenarios all describe the same integrated 2D explorer.

subViews.description: A 2D concept-space view combining colored cluster regions, cluster labels, highlighted relevant features, intersection points, and a query point marker.

quotes: The system should provide an intuitive spatial representation of features.
Hierarchical clustering results are displayed as a color-coded map with representative topic terms at cluster centers.
We employ HexBin techniques.
evidence.reasoning: The explorer is one composite visualization with no justified internal boundary beyond overlays in the same map.

x.description: Horizontal position in the 2D semantic space.

y.description: Vertical position in the 2D semantic space.

color.description: Color family distinguishes cluster regions and hierarchy levels.

x.description: Feature position in the concept-space map.

y.description: Feature position in the concept-space map.

color.description: Visual emphasis distinguishes highlighted relevant features from other feature points.

x.description: Horizontal position of the query pin or related guide marker.

y.description: Vertical position of the query pin or related guide marker.

color.description: Color highlights the query marker relative to other guide markers.

x.description: Horizontal position of a representative cluster label.

y.description: Vertical position of a representative cluster label.

text.description: Representative topic terms shown at cluster centers.

quotes: All feature explanation vectors are mapped to a two-dimensional semantic space.
Hierarchical clustering results are displayed as a color-coded map.
We employ HexBin techniques.
evidence.reasoning: The crop clearly shows hexagonal regions, point overlays, labels, and query/intersection markers.

processingSteps.name: UMAP projection
processingSteps.description: Projects 3072-dimensional feature explanation embeddings into a 2D semantic layout for global browsing.

processingSteps.name: Hierarchical semantic clustering
processingSteps.description: Groups features into multi-level semantic clusters and derives representative topic labels for exploration at different granularities.

quotes: We apply UMAP to reduce the 3072-dimensional feature embeddings to a 2D space.
We employ Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering at multiple granularity levels.
evidence.reasoning: These methods directly produce the explorer's projection and cluster structure.

capabilities.description: Shows how features distribute across semantic regions and cluster levels in the concept space.

quotes: The system should provide an intuitive spatial representation of features.
Starting at a high-level view, user identify broad conceptual areas.
evidence.reasoning: The explorer supports overview-level semantic navigation.

capabilities.description: Move between clustering granularities to inspect coarse and fine semantic groupings.

quotes: Level transitions through zooming, offering multi-granularity semantic views.
Drilling down into the scientific region reveals intermediate clusters.
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly describes zoom-driven level transitions.

capabilities.description: Choose a feature point for detailed semantic inspection and validation.

quotes: The user selects this point for detailed analysis.
Feature 6610 captures the user's attention.
evidence.reasoning: Feature selection is the primary handoff from the explorer to detail and validation views.

capabilities.description: Displays bubble-set or proximity-based indications of other features with similar activation behavior or nearby semantics.

quotes: Highlighting bubble sets displayed in the Feature Explorer View.
Feature 9638's proximity to feature 6610 in the feature space captures the user's attention.
evidence.reasoning: The explorer receives related-feature highlights and exposes spatial proximity cues.

viewsInfo.description: Detail view for interpreting a selected feature through vocabulary-space evidence and activation-similarity examples.

quotes: Feature Details View presents comprehensive information about selected features.
The left panel (D1) shows the feature's projection in vocabulary space.
The right panel (D2) displays an activation-similarity matrix.
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly names the two internal panels and explains their complementary roles.

subViews.description: Left panel showing vocabulary projections for the selected feature, with words on positive and negative logit sides.

quotes: The left panel (D1) shows the feature's projection in vocabulary space, mapping words with the highest and lowest probabilities.
The Feature Details View displays vocabulary space projections.
evidence.reasoning: D1 is explicitly identified and described in the paper and visible in the crop.

y.description: Vocabulary items associated with the selected feature.

x.description: Strength of positive or negative logit projection for each token.

color.description: Distinguishes negative logits from positive logits.

text.description: Rendered token labels beside the bars.

quotes: Mapping words with the highest and lowest probabilities.
evidence.reasoning: The panel is rendered as two lists of horizontal bars with token labels.

capabilities.description: Shows which vocabulary items are most positively or negatively associated with the selected feature.

quotes: Mapping words with the highest and lowest probabilities.
evidence.reasoning: This is the explicit purpose of D1.

subViews.description: Right panel centered on the activation-similarity matrix for sampled text segments, with interaction for inspection and sentence-level details.

quotes: The right panel (D2) displays an activation-similarity matrix of sampled text segments.
Users can also view complete sentences for context through the View Details option.
evidence.reasoning: D2 combines the matrix and its accompanying segment detail affordances.

x.description: Sentence ranking by semantic similarity to the feature explanation.

y.description: Sentence ranking by maximum activation value.

color.description: Background or cell color indicates activation intensity regions across the matrix.

quotes: The x-axis represents the ranking of sentences by semantic similarity and the y-axis represents their ranking by maximum activation value.
evidence.reasoning: The D2 panel is explicitly described as the activation-similarity matrix view.

processingSteps.name: Activation-similarity comparison
processingSteps.description: Compares each sampled sentence's maximum activation value with the cosine similarity between the sentence embedding and the feature explanation embedding.

quotes: We compare each sampled sentence's maximum activation value with the cosine similarity between the sentence's text embedding and the feature explanation embedding.
evidence.reasoning: The matrix view directly operationalizes this comparison.

capabilities.description: Shows where sampled segments align or diverge between activation strength and semantic similarity.

quotes: This multi-faceted approach reveals discrepancies between explanations and actual feature behavior.
evidence.reasoning: The matrix is designed to expose alignment and anomalous cases.

capabilities.description: Select a region of the activation-similarity matrix to update token summaries and inspect supporting details.

quotes: When users select regions in the matrix, the bar chart updates to show token distributions from the selected segments.
evidence.reasoning: This is the paper's only explicit interaction inside D2.

capabilities.description: Open complete sentences for the selected examples to inspect context around feature activations.

quotes: Users can also view complete sentences for context through the View Details option.
evidence.reasoning: The detail-on-demand action is explicitly named.

subViews.description: Adjacent summary chart showing the maximum-activation tokens for sampled or selected text segments.

quotes: Adjacent to this matrix, a bar chart summarizes the maximum activation tokens for each text segment.
The Feature Details View displays vocabulary space projections and maximum token activation statistics.
evidence.reasoning: The paper describes the token summary as a separate adjacent chart, so it is modeled as its own visual sub-view.

y.description: Tokens summarized for the current selection or segment set.

x.description: Summary magnitude or count associated with each token.

quotes: Adjacent to this matrix, a bar chart summarizes the maximum activation tokens for each text segment.
evidence.reasoning: The adjacent summary is explicitly described as a bar chart.

capabilities.description: Shows token-level activation statistics for the current set of sampled or selected text segments.

quotes: A bar chart summarizes the maximum activation tokens for each text segment.
evidence.reasoning: This summary chart provides token-level information complementary to the matrix.

viewsInfo.description: Validation view for testing the selected feature on custom input text and inspecting token-level activations.

quotes: Input Activation View allows users to verify feature responses to custom inputs.
The system displays the activation distribution across tokens for the target feature.
evidence.reasoning: The paper and crop consistently show a prompt entry area paired with token-level activation results.

subViews.description: Input field used to submit custom text samples for activation analysis on the selected feature.

quotes: Users can enter text samples.
The user proceeds to the Custom Input Activation View, inputting text containing token plant.
evidence.reasoning: The field and add button at the top of the crop support custom text submission.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Text-entry control for custom sentences or prompts used in token-level activation validation.

quotes: When users input text, the system performs a forward pass through the model.
evidence.reasoning: This region is a text entry form, not a plotted display.

capabilities.description: Submit a sentence or prompt to test how the selected feature responds in context.

quotes: Users can enter text samples.
The user tests this feature with various superhero-related prompts.
evidence.reasoning: This is the primary interaction supported by the prompt input box.

subViews.description: Token-by-token readout showing which words in the submitted text strongly activate the selected feature and which tokens can be used to retrieve related features.

quotes: The system displays the activation distribution across tokens for the target feature.
For selected tokens with high activation, the system recommends other features with similar activation patterns.
evidence.reasoning: The crop shows token chips with highlighted high-activation terms; the paper explains the same interaction.

x.description: Sequential token position in the submitted text.

row.description: Separate tested phrases or text lines.

color.description: Visual emphasis communicates which tokens strongly activate the selected feature.

quotes: Records the activation strength of target features at each token position.
evidence.reasoning: The token display is best modeled as a text-mark view with activation-driven emphasis.

processingSteps.name: Custom input activation analysis
processingSteps.description: Runs the selected text through the model, maps intermediate activations to SAE feature space, and records target-feature activation strength for each token.

processingSteps.name: Co-activation feature retrieval
processingSteps.description: Finds other features that strongly activate for user-selected tokens in order to surface functionally related features.
output: related features with similar activation patterns

quotes: We implemented this method for user-defined text.
We provide a co-activation feature retrieval function.
evidence.reasoning: These methods directly power the token display and related-feature retrieval behavior.

capabilities.description: Shows which tokens in the submitted text trigger the selected feature most strongly.

quotes: The system displays the activation distribution across tokens for the target feature.
evidence.reasoning: Token-level inspection is the core information affordance of the view.

capabilities.description: Select highly activated tokens to retrieve other features with similar activation behavior.

quotes: For selected tokens with high activation, the system recommends other features.
The user then selects superhero tokens to identify other features with similar activation patterns.
evidence.reasoning: This action drives the explicit coordination back to the explorer.

viewsInfo.description: Causal-validation view for comparing generated outputs under different feature activation strengths.

quotes: Output Steering View enables causal verification of feature-concept relationships.
Generate multiple output variants with different feature activation strengths simultaneously.
evidence.reasoning: The crop shows prompt entry and multiple steered branches, matching the text.

subViews.description: Prompt-entry field and generate trigger used to launch activation steering experiments.

quotes: The Activation Steer module allows users to input prompts.
The user employs the Activation Steering View with the prompt.
evidence.reasoning: The upper field and generate button are a separate control region in the crop.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Prompt input used to generate steered language model outputs for the currently selected feature.

quotes: Enable users to dynamically adjust input samples and feature activations while observing resulting changes.
evidence.reasoning: This region provides prompt submission rather than direct data visualization.

capabilities.description: Provide a prompt for causal steering experiments and trigger generation.

quotes: The Activation Steer module allows users to input prompts.
evidence.reasoning: The prompt field is the explicit entry point for steering experiments.

subViews.description: Generated output panels showing multiple completions under different steering strengths for side-by-side comparison.

quotes: Generate multiple output variants with different feature activation strengths simultaneously.
By comparing outputs across different steering branches, users can observe how manipulating feature activations affects model outputs.
evidence.reasoning: The crop clearly shows multiple output cards branching from the same input prompt.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: A card-based set of generated text responses used to compare the causal effects of different steering strengths.

quotes: This method allows users to observe the causal impact of feature activation changes on model output.
evidence.reasoning: The outputs are text cards rather than a plotted chart.

processingSteps.name: Activation steering
processingSteps.description: Applies a steering vector derived from the SAE decoder to increase or suppress a selected feature during model inference.

quotes: We extract the steering vector for a feature from SAE.
Positive values enhance feature influence, negative values suppress it.
evidence.reasoning: The output-branch panels are produced by the steering mechanism described in the methods section.

capabilities.description: Shows how different steering strengths change generated text so users can assess causal feature-concept effects.

quotes: By comparing outputs with and without steering, users can verify the association.
By comparing outputs across different steering branches.
evidence.reasoning: The information affordance of the output cards is side-by-side causal comparison.

quotes: The Concept Query View and SAE Discovery View help users locate SAE models with features related to target concepts.
After finalizing the query, the user examines the SAE Model Retrieval View.
The system highlights query-relevant features.
evidence.reasoning: Submitting or refining the query updates both the layer relevance results and the highlighted relevant features in the explorer.

quotes: Users can directly select the SAE of interest from this view to proceed with feature exploration.

evidence.reasoning: Selecting a layer-specific SAE updates the feature space shown in the explorer.

quotes: The user selects this point for detailed analysis.
The Feature Details View displays vocabulary space projections and maximum token activation statistics.

quotes: When users select regions in the matrix, the bar chart updates to show token distributions from the selected segments.

quotes: For selected tokens with high activation, the system recommends other features with similar activation patterns by highlighting bubble sets displayed in the Feature Explorer View.
The user then selects superhero tokens to identify other features with similar activation patterns.
evidence.reasoning: Selecting activated tokens in the validation view highlights related features in the explorer.

quotes: Users can enter text samples, and the system displays the activation distribution across tokens for the target feature.
The user proceeds to the Custom Input Activation View, inputting text containing token plant.

quotes: The Activation Steer module allows users to input prompts and generate multiple output variants with different feature activation strengths simultaneously.
The user employs the Output Steering View with the prompt.
evidence.reasoning: Entering a steering prompt and triggering generation updates the output-branch panel within the steering view.

quotes: Input Activation View allows users to verify feature responses to custom inputs.
Output Steering View enables causal verification of feature-concept relationships.
The user selects this point for detailed analysis.
